Content_Sketch: Born on the top floor of a Manhattan tower — the entire floor belongs to you
Illustration_Note: First-person view from penthouse window — Manhattan skyline below, stickman hands on glass

Content_Sketch: Your name appears in newspapers before you can speak
Illustration_Note: Dark navy background — bold text: BORN INTO EVERYTHING.

Content_Sketch: You didn't choose this family. This family chose you.
Illustration_Note: Dark background — text: YOU DIDN'T CHOOSE THIS FAMILY. — small stickman silhouette bottom-right

Content_Sketch: You are four. You speak two languages — your father's and your mother's homeland tongue.
Illustration_Note: Small stickman in gold-toned penthouse living room — tall windows, city lights

Content_Sketch: You are seven. Black SUVs take you to a private school in Manhattan. Your friends are background-checked before they can come to your birthday.
Illustration_Note: Small stickman with backpack walking toward school entrance — black SUV behind, agent figure standing

Content_Sketch: You use a different name at school. No one knows which family you belong to.
Illustration_Note: Left: stickman labeled 'Jack' among classmates / Right: same stickman in penthouse tower — two lives

Content_Sketch: You are ten. Your father wins. You move into a house with one hundred thirty-two rooms. None of them feel like yours.
Illustration_Note: Left panel AGE 7 stickman in Manhattan school / Right panel AGE 10 stickman in White House hallway

Content_Sketch: Your mother stays behind in New York for a few months. So you can finish the school year. She chose you over the spotlight.
Illustration_Note: Small stickman looking out White House window — warm amber tint fading at edges

Content_Sketch: From here on — everything changes.
Illustration_Note: White text on dark background: FROM HERE ON. EVERYTHING CHANGES.

Content_Sketch: Secret Service agents sit outside your classroom in Maryland. They wear plain clothes but everyone knows.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman at school desk — two dark-suited figures visible through classroom door window

Content_Sketch: Your friends want to visit. They need to pass a federal background check first. Some stop trying.
Illustration_Note: Left: stickman alone in White House corridor / Right: group of stickmen hanging out on suburban street

Content_Sketch: Your siblings go on television. They argue. They campaign. They become public figures. You stay silent.
Illustration_Note: Living room — TV screen showing multiple small stickmen. Subject stickman sits alone on couch, watching

Content_Sketch: Your father is impeached for the first time. You are thirteen. You read it on your phone in your bedroom.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman sitting on bed, hunched over phone — cool blue wash at edges

Content_Sketch: You are fourteen. The family leaves Washington for Florida. New school. New state. New version of you.
Illustration_Note: Left panel: White House silhouette / Right panel: palm trees and gated estate

Content_Sketch: You are seventeen. Over six foot seven. You cannot hide anymore. Not in a crowd. Not anywhere.
Illustration_Note: Very tall stickman walking through school hallway — noticeably taller than every other figure

Content_Sketch: People photograph you walking. Just walking. That is enough.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman walking on sidewalk — tiny phone-holding stickmen in background. Desaturated grey tint

Content_Sketch: You are eighteen. You graduate. Your father is running again.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman in graduation cap — campaign signs visible in background landscape

Content_Sketch: You are invited to be a delegate at the party convention. You say no.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman standing alone in empty room — phone on table. Cool grey tint

Content_Sketch: Inauguration day. Second time. You stand on the stage. You are taller than everyone. The cameras find you every time.
Illustration_Note: First-person view from inauguration stage — crowd stretching to horizon, flags, columns of marble

Content_Sketch: The whole world is watching you. And you haven't said a word.
Illustration_Note: Dark background — THE WORLD IS WATCHING. / AND YOU HAVEN'T SAID A WORD.

Content_Sketch: You are nineteen. You enroll in a business school in New York. Back to Manhattan. Back to where you were born.
Illustration_Note: Left panel: inauguration stage / Right panel: Manhattan campus building

Content_Sketch: Secret Service still follows you into lecture halls. Stands outside your door at night.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman in lecture hall — agent figure standing at back wall. Other student stickmen seated

Content_Sketch: But for the first time you chose the school. You chose the city. You chose business — not law. Not politics.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman walking across campus — soft gold light from above. Slight smile implied by posture

Content_Sketch: It is not freedom. But it is the closest you have ever had.
Illustration_Note: Warm amber background — IT'S NOT FREEDOM. / BUT IT'S THE CLOSEST YOU'VE HAD.

Content_Sketch: You are twenty. You transfer to the campus in Washington. You move back into the old house. This time it is your choice.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman walking through White House entrance — same building, different posture — confident

Content_Sketch: Your father calls you the king of the internet. You are the one who brought the campaign to podcasters and streamers.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman in office — screens showing podcast icons, headphone graphics on wall

Content_Sketch: You have no social media accounts. But millions of people talk about you online every day.
Illustration_Note: Left: stickman with no phone, sitting calmly / Right: wall of floating comment bubbles and phone screens

Content_Sketch: Fake accounts use your name. Doctored photos circulate. Dating rumors are fabricated. You say nothing.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman sitting at desk — phone face-down on table. Desaturated cold grey

Content_Sketch: You start appearing in business meetings. Crypto. Beverages. Real estate. You are building something — quietly.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman at conference table — smaller stickmen across table, documents between them

Content_Sketch: You attend the State dinner. Front row. Taller than every senator in the room.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman in Congress chamber — marble walls, flag behind podium, seated stickmen below eye level

Content_Sketch: You still have not spoken publicly. But your silence became a brand.
Illustration_Note: Dark navy — YOUR SILENCE BECAME A BRAND.

Content_Sketch: Remember the penthouse? The tower in Manhattan? The entire floor that was yours?
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman standing in penthouse — same room as BEAT_01_01, but stickman now fills the frame

Content_Sketch: The whole floor was yours. But you never really lived there.
Illustration_Note: Tall stickman at penthouse window — Manhattan skyline behind. Soft warm white edges

Content_Sketch: You are twenty. And you still have not decided who you are.
Illustration_Note: Dark background — YOU ARE TWENTY. / AND YOU STILL HAVEN'T DECIDED WHO YOU ARE.

Content_Sketch: But maybe that is the one right no one can take from you.
Illustration_Note: Warm amber background — MAYBE THAT'S THE ONE RIGHT / NO ONE CAN TAKE FROM YOU.
